# Pureflow Architecture Guide

## Audience

This guide is for engineers who need a system-level understanding of Pureflow.

It is written for humans first. The point is not to compress the design into a
set of symbols for tooling; the point is to explain why the system is shaped
the way it is, what each layer is protecting, and what tradeoffs are worth
remembering when the code changes.

## Scope

- High-level architecture and subsystem responsibilities.
- Runtime flow and execution model.
- Package and contract boundaries.
- Diagram-backed explanations for key pathways.

## Recommended Reading Order

If you are new to the codebase, read this guide in order:

1. [System Overview](system-overview.qmd) for the big picture and the main
   design choices.
2. [Runtime Model](runtime-model.qmd) for how work actually moves through the
   engine.
3. [Package and Contract Boundaries](package-contracts.qmd) for why the crates
   are split the way they are.
4. [Diagrams and Flows](diagrams-and-flows.qmd) for the conventions behind the
   figures.

## Why This Guide Exists

Pureflow has enough moving parts that “what the code does” is not the same as
“why the code is arranged this way.” This guide is here to bridge that gap for
the next person who opens the repo with coffee in hand and wants the short
version before they start reading Rust.

## Relationship to Other Docs

- API reference is generated separately via `cargo doc`.
- Retrieval-oriented facts live in `docs/llm/`.
- Cross-repo reuse proposal for `highland-labs`:
  [highland-labs-integration-proposal.md](../archetecture/highland-labs-integration-proposal.md)
- crates.io publishing options and recommendation:
  [crates-io-publishing-options.md](../archetecture/crates-io-publishing-options.md)
